title: Crispy Floral Lebanese Lamb Tagine
description: A lazySunday Middle Eastern lamb tagine that balances the aromatic floral notes of rose water and currants with a crispy, caramelized crust.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, bone-in, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
Middle Eastern spice blend (cumin, coriander, cinnamon, turmeric)

instructions: Pat the lamb cubes dry with paper towels and season with salt and pepper.
Heat 2 tbsp of olive oil in a heavy, oven‑safe tagine or Dutch oven over medium‑high heat. Sear the lamb until all sides are browned, about 4–5 minutes per batch. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp of oil to the pot. Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 5 minutes, then stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the Middle Eastern spice blend to the pot and toast for 1 minute, stirring constantly to temper the spices.
Return the browned lamb to the pot and stir to coat. Add the currants, lemon juice, and rosemary sprigs. Pour enough water to just cover the lamb (about 300 ml).
Bring the liquid to a gentle simmer, cover, and reduce the heat to low. Let the tagine cook for 2 hours, or until the lamb is fork‑tender.
Preheat the oven’s broiler. Transfer the tagine contents to a shallow baking dish if not already in one, spreading the lamb and sauce out for even crisping.
Broil for 3–4 minutes, watching carefully until the top attains a golden‑brown, crispy crust.
Remove from the oven, drizzle with rose water, sprinkle chopped parsley and a handful of extra currants, and serve immediately with couscous or flatbread.
